theme_context: This week, Google I/O and Anthropic's Code with Claude crowned agents as the dominant 2026 paradigm, while DeepSeek's permanent 75% V4-Pro price cut openly weaponized cost as a competitive lever. Underneath, Sequoia's "Services: The New Software" thesis is now the unified investor playbook, a16z is deploying across vertical autopilots and creative infra, and frontier-lab leaders are simultaneously trumpeting AGI proximity ("foothills of the singularity") and warning about compute scarcity. The strategic question for operators is no longer "which model" but "which orchestration layer, which vertical, and at what unit economics."

items.title: Services Are The New Software
items.summary: Sequoia's hardened 2026 thesis: for every $1 spent on software, $6 is spent on services, and AI-native 'autopilots' that sell completed work (closed books, settled tickets, drafted contracts) — not tools — will capture a ~$10T market. Implication for capital: copilot SaaS is structurally short, vertical autopilots with outcome pricing are structurally long, and incumbents face an innovator's-dilemma trap if they cannibalize their tool revenue.

items.title: 2026 Is The Year of Agents
items.summary: At AI Ascent 2026, Sonya Huang declared agents have crossed from useless to production-grade because models, tools, and 'harnesses' (orchestration) finally converged; Pat Grady framed today's state as functionally indistinguishable from AGI for commercial purposes. Implication: capital is rotating from pure-model bets to harness/orchestration layers and to companies exploiting the 'diffusion gap' between frontier capability and Fortune 500 deployment.

items.title: Open Source AI As National Strategy
items.summary: Ben Horowitz's argument: the US lost the open-source AI cultural layer to China via DeepSeek and similar models that now run inside US enterprises and labs; closed-model policy locked American innovation behind walls while Beijing-aligned weights spread globally. Implication: a16z is positioning for decentralized/open-weight AI infra, sovereign-AI plays, and US-domiciled open-source labs as a policy-aligned investable wedge.

items.title: Compute Is The Binding Constraint
items.summary: Brockman (OpenAI), echoed by Sequoia and DeepMind, frames 2026 as compute-scarce: capability scales with compute, GPU availability 'rounds to zero,' and memory is now ~two-thirds of AI chip BOM. Implication: capital flows are bifurcating toward compute (data centers, memory, custom silicon, power) and toward token-efficient architectures, while application-layer founders must design for cost-per-token volatility.

items.title: AI Search & Brand Visibility As A New Category
items.summary: Berlin's Peec more than doubled ARR to $10M in months helping brands monitor and steer their presence inside AI answers — proof that 'GEO/AEO' (generative engine optimization) is becoming a defensible SaaS category as ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity displace blue-link search. Implication: a near-term enterprise budget shift from SEO/PR analytics to AI-visibility tooling, with European challengers credibly leading.

items.title: Anthropic's Code with Claude: agentic coding as default
items.summary: Anthropic's London developer event positioned Claude Code as the orchestration surface for AI engineering, with attendees reporting whole PRs written autonomously. Strategic read: Anthropic is consolidating the coding-agent layer as its primary enterprise wedge while DeepSeek and OpenAI compete on price and reasoning — the moat is now memory, integrations, and trust, not the underlying model.

items.title: Google I/O: Gemini, Antigravity, and 'foothills of the singularity'
items.summary: Google pushed agents into search, workspace, and scientific discovery (WeatherNext, Isomorphic), with Antigravity 2.0 demoed building an OS for under $1,000. Strategic read: Google is operationalizing the services-thesis at full-stack scale — model + harness + distribution — and forcing every application-layer startup to answer 'what survives when Gemini ships your feature?'

items.title: DeepSeek V4-Pro 75% price cut made permanent
items.summary: DeepSeek locked in V4-Pro at ~$0.435/M input and $0.87/M output tokens — roughly 11-34x cheaper than GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7 — backed by Huawei Ascend 950 supply. Strategic read: the era of high-margin frontier tokens is ending; agentic workloads (which burn 10-100x more tokens than chat) will increasingly route to DeepSeek for non-sensitive tasks, compressing Western lab gross margins and accelerating commoditization.

items.title: Stitch 3.0 by Google (a16z-backed design-to-code)
items.summary: Validates 2026 Is The Year of Agents: Stitch 3.0 turns natural-language prompts into production UI + code, operationalizing the 'harness' layer Sequoia describes — model + tools + workflow stitched into a delivered artifact rather than a chat reply. a16z's parallel investment in Stitch signals capital conviction that design-to-deployed-software is the next agent-native wedge.

items.title: Runway Agent
items.summary: Validates Services Are The New Software: Runway Agent sells finished creative output (edits, sequences, motion design) rather than a video tool, mapping directly onto the autopilot vs. copilot framing — customers buy the work, not the timeline. Signals that creative services ($200B+ globally) is one of the next verticals to flip from tool-sales to outcome-sales.

items.title: ModelHub
items.summary: Validates Compute Is The Binding Constraint: ModelHub gives teams a unified routing/observability layer across frontier and open-weight models, letting them arbitrage cost (DeepSeek), latency, and capability per task. Operationalizes the bet that in a price-war world, the orchestration/routing tier captures margin that the model layer is losing.

items.title: AI brand-visibility tooling for mid-market
items.summary: Peec proves there is enterprise willingness-to-pay for tracking and influencing brand presence inside AI answers. Who captures: a US-domiciled or vertical-specialized challenger targeting B2B SaaS, ecommerce, or pharma marketing teams. What must be true: that 'AI overviews' continue to displace 10-15%+ of organic search clicks. When: this quarter — budgets are being reallocated mid-year.

items.title: DeepSeek-arbitrage agentic workloads
items.summary: Validates Compute Is The Binding Constraint: With output tokens ~34x cheaper than GPT-5.5, agent builders can profitably run 10-step+ workflows on DeepSeek for non-sensitive tasks (research, content, code scaffolding). Who captures: agent startups willing to dual-route by sensitivity tier. What must be true: that geopolitical/data-residency objections can be addressed via hybrid deployment. When: 0-3 months — the unit economics gap is open right now.

items.title: n8n as the agent-orchestration wedge for SMB
items.summary: Validates 2026 Is The Year of Agents: n8n's open-source workflow + AI nodes give SMBs a buildable autopilot stack without enterprise-platform pricing, capturing the diffusion gap Sequoia calls out. Who captures: agencies and indie operators packaging vertical autopilots on top of n8n. What must be true: that no-code orchestration remains 'good enough' vs. native frameworks. When: this quarter.

items.title: Vertical autopilots in regulated services (legal, accounting, claims)
items.summary: Sequoia's autopilot wave (Crosby, Rillet, Auctor) shows the playbook: pick a $50B+ services vertical where work is verifiable, sell outcomes priced against headcount. Who captures: domain-deep founders with an outsourcing/BPO wedge. What must be true: that judgement-to-intelligence task migration continues with each model release. When: the next 6 months are the window before incumbents wake up.

items.title: Memory-tier silicon and HBM-adjacent plays
items.summary: Memory is now ~two-thirds of AI chip component cost, restructuring who captures value in the compute supply chain. Who captures: HBM packaging specialists, CXL/memory-pooling startups, and inference-optimized memory architectures. What must be true: that long-context and agentic workloads continue to expand KV-cache footprints. When: 6-18 months as next-gen accelerators tape out.

items.title: AI wealth management and personal financial autopilots
items.summary: Nevis brings AI to wealth management — a $100T AUM industry where advisors charge 1% to do work agents can now perform. Who captures: AI-native RIAs and B2B copilots-turning-autopilots for independent advisors. What must be true: regulators allow agentic execution under fiduciary frameworks. When: 12-18 months as first compliant agents reach production.

items.title: World models for embodied AI and simulation
items.summary: World models are emerging as the architecture beyond LLMs — Google already uses Genie inside Maps Street View. Who captures: robotics, autonomy, and simulation startups that can train on physics-aware synthetic data. What must be true: world models scale beyond demos to drive real downstream task performance. When: 12-24 months as foundation world models become commercially licensable.

items.title: Sovereign / open-weight enterprise stacks
items.summary: If Horowitz is right that closed-only US policy ceded the cultural layer to DeepSeek, enterprises and governments will demand open-weight, on-prem alternatives. Who captures: US open-source labs, sovereign-cloud integrators, and fine-tuning platforms targeting defense/finance/healthcare. What must be true: a policy shift permitting open-weight export and procurement. When: 12-18 months post-election cycle.
items.aligned_thesis: Open Source AI As National Strategy

items.title: AI-driven scientific discovery as a venture asset class
items.summary: Hassabis frames Isomorphic Labs as a technology that could 'cure hundreds of diseases,' and Sequoia/OpenAI are citing Erdős-class math solved by agents. Who captures: AI-first biotech, materials, and theorem-proving labs with proprietary feedback loops. What must be true: discovery agents produce IP-defensible breakthroughs at lower cost than incumbent R&D. When: 24-60 months — the first IPO of a pure 'AI-discovery' company will reprice the category.

items.title: Orbital and off-grid AI compute
items.summary: SpaceX is openly pursuing orbital data centers while xAI loads up on natural-gas peakers — a directional signal that terrestrial grid limits will force compute off-planet or onto dedicated generation. Who captures: launch-cost optimizers, space-grade cooling/power, and behind-the-meter gas/nuclear-for-AI developers. What must be true: launch costs fall another 5-10x and orbital thermal management scales. When: 36-60 months for first commercial orbital inference.

items.title: Agent-managed personal economies
items.summary: Brockman's framing of humans as 'managers of agents' and Sequoia's consumer autopilot vision (inbox, calendar, finances, taxes) point to a long-arc shift where individuals run agent stacks the way SMBs run software stacks. Who captures: consumer-agent OS, identity/auth for agents, and agent-to-agent payment rails. What must be true: trust, liability, and UX problems are solved for non-technical users. When: 24-48 months for first 10M-user consumer autopilot.

leader_voices.name: Demis Hassabis
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Closing the Google I/O 2026 keynote on May 19, Hassabis told the audience that when we look back at this period we will realize we were 'standing in the foothills of the singularity,' framing agentic systems and Gemini/Omni updates as a turning point and reiterating his view that AGI-class systems could arrive by around 2030.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: When the most restrained frontier-lab CEO publicly anchors AGI within ~5 years and ties it to shipped products (Antigravity, WeatherNext, Isomorphic), enterprise buyers will accelerate 2026 AI budgets and regulators will accelerate scrutiny — both compress decision windows for founders.

leader_voices.name: Greg Brockman
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: On The Knowledge Project and at Sequoia AI Ascent 2026, Brockman said it is 'hard to know what percent is not' written by AI inside OpenAI, estimated AGI is roughly 70-80% there by his definition, and characterized compute as the binding constraint — a 'revenue centre, not a cost centre' to which OpenAI is committing essentially all available capital.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: Brockman is publicly justifying OpenAI's $122B 2026 capital deployment via AI-coding productivity claims; if internalized by enterprise CTOs, this accelerates engineer-replacement narratives and pressures every dev-tools startup to prove agentic, not assistive, value.

leader_voices.name: Sonya Huang
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: At AI Ascent 2026, Huang declared that if 2022-2024 was chat and 2024-2025 was reasoning, 2026 is the year of agents — pursuing goals rather than answering prompts — and cited compressed-timeline anecdotes including Notion rewriting 8M lines of code in six weeks and Bret Taylor rebuilding Sierra over a weekend.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: Sequoia is signaling that 2026 deal flow will prioritize agent-native architectures and long-horizon harnesses; founders pitching copilots without an autopilot roadmap will see term sheets reprice or disappear.

leader_voices.name: Julien Bek
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Bek's viral 'Services: The New Software' framing — that for every $1 spent on software, $6 is spent on services, and the next trillion-dollar company will sell completed work, not tools — is now the organizing thesis cited across Sequoia portfolio investments like Auctor, Crosby, and Rillet.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: Pricing models, GTM, and team composition all change under this thesis: founders must choose explicitly between tool, copilot, agent, or AI-native service — and investors will increasingly reject pitches that 'will become a feature in the next model release.'

leader_voices.name: Ben Horowitz
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Horowitz argues the US already lost the AI cultural war to China because closed-model policy locked American innovation behind walls while Chinese open-weight models like DeepSeek became the default inside US enterprises and university labs, encoding non-Western values into billions of downstream devices.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: a16z is publicly building a policy case for open-weight US labs and decentralized AI — expect deployment into open-source foundation models, sovereign-AI integrators, and lobbying-adjacent infra; founders aligned with this narrative gain a clear capital channel.

leader_voices.name: Yann LeCun
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Counter to Hassabis's 'foothills of the singularity' framing, LeCun reiterated this week that current LLM-based systems are not genuinely intelligent and lack the ability to learn from experience and produce real breakthroughs — a missing capability he says world models are needed to address.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: The public Hassabis/LeCun split gives enterprise buyers and risk officers cover to slow agentic deployments pending world-model maturity — operators should plan for a bifurcated 12-month market where some buyers race and others freeze.

leader_voices.name: Pat Grady
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: Opening AI Ascent 2026, Grady framed AI as a revolution in computation — 'not faster horses, but cars' — and called the current state functionally indistinguishable from AGI for commercial purposes, advising founders to 'get MAD,' build moats from the customer back, and exploit the diffusion gap between model capability and Fortune 500 deployment.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: The diffusion-gap framing is now an explicit Sequoia investment filter — startups whose only edge is access to a frontier model will be passed over in favor of those whose moat is customer workflow integration, change management, and verticalized eval/feedback loops.

leader_voices.name: Clément Delangue
leader_voices.quote_or_paragraph: In a recent interview re-aired on the a16z Show, Delangue argued that the real AI bubble is in API-based large language models — not open-source — and predicted that robotics will become the next major interface for AI as open-weight models continue to commoditize text and code.
leader_voices.strategic_implication: If Delangue is right, mid-2020s API-revenue multiples are mispriced and capital will rotate toward open-weight + robotics; founders should stress-test their reliance on closed-API margins and look at embodied AI as the next defensible distribution layer.

top_signals.headline: DeepSeek makes 75% V4-Pro discount permanent — ~34x cheaper output tokens than GPT-5.5
top_signals.why_it_matters: Permanently resets the unit economics of agentic AI: any operator running multi-step agents on Western frontier models is now structurally over-paying for non-sensitive workloads. Repricing pressure on OpenAI/Anthropic gross margins begins this quarter; expect retaliatory price cuts and a hard split between sensitive (Western) and bulk (Chinese) inference routing.

top_signals.headline: Sequoia + Google + Anthropic converge: 2026 is the year of agents
top_signals.why_it_matters: When the dominant VC, the dominant search company, and the dominant coding lab independently declare agents production-ready in the same week, capital allocation and enterprise procurement follow within one quarter. Founders without an explicit autopilot/outcome-pricing story will face term-sheet repricing.

top_signals.headline: Hassabis publicly anchors AGI timeline at 'foothills of the singularity'
top_signals.why_it_matters: DeepMind's CEO moving from restraint to AGI-imminence rhetoric will pull regulatory attention forward (EU/UK hearings, US executive action) and pull enterprise AI budgets forward simultaneously. Both effects compress strategic decision windows — particularly for AI safety/governance startups and sovereign-AI plays.

top_signals.headline: Memory hits ~two-thirds of AI chip component cost
top_signals.why_it_matters: The bottleneck in scaling agentic and long-context workloads is shifting from compute to memory — restructuring who wins in silicon. HBM packaging specialists, CXL/memory-pooling startups, and inference-optimized architectures become higher-priority investments than another GPU SKU.

top_signals.headline: Peec hits $10M ARR tracking brand presence in AI answers
top_signals.why_it_matters: First clean public data point that 'AI-search optimization' is a real, fast-growing SaaS category — proof that marketing budgets are mid-cycle reallocating from SEO/PR analytics. Operators should evaluate AI-visibility tooling in this planning cycle; investors should expect a flurry of US challengers within 90 days.
